channel.title: Frontion News - Global Geopolitics Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 China blocks Pentagon official visit over Taiwan arms package
item.description: Beijing refused to approve a visit by Pentagon Under-Secretary Elbridge Colby in protest of a $14 billion US weapons package for Taiwan, escalating US-China tensions.

item.title: 🇬🇧💰 UK inflation cools as Iran war costs loom
item.description: British inflation fell more than expected to 2.8% in April, but the relief may be temporary as economic impacts from the Iran conflict are expected to hit households later this year.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇺 US charges Raúl Castro with murder over 1996 plane shootdown
item.description: The Justice Department unsealed an indictment charging former Cuban President Raúl Castro with murder for the 1996 downing of two civilian planes operated by Cuban exiles, amid escalating US pressure on Cuba.

item.title: ⚔️🇷🇺 UK reports dangerous Russian jet intercept over Black Sea
item.description: Britain defense ministry said two Russian jets dangerously intercepted a RAF surveillance plane over the Black Sea last month, raising concerns about potential NATO-Russia escalation.

item.title: 🇨🇩🦠 WHO warns Ebola outbreak growing in Congo
item.description: The World Health Organization reports 139 suspected deaths and 600 cases from a rare Bundibugyo Ebola strain in DRC, with no approved vaccine and outbreak expected to expand.

item.title: 🏛️ Senate Advances Iran War Powers Resolution as Republican Cassidy Flips
item.description: The US Senate voted 50-47 to advance legislation forcing Trump to withdraw from the Iran war, with Louisiana Senator Bill Cassidy joining Democrats after his primary loss. The vote signals growing Republican unease with the prolonged conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇷🇺 Xi and Putin Meet in Beijing Days After Trump Visit, Sign 40 Cooperation Agreements
item.description: Chinese and Russian leaders hailed their strategic ties and growing energy trade, signing over 40 agreements in technology, trade, and media. Xi called ties "the highest level in history" but no progress was made on the Power of Siberia 2 gas pipeline Russia had pushed for.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚔️ Iran Displays Weapons at Public Events as Tehran Readies for Potential War Resumption
item.description: Iranian Revolutionary Guard members are conducting public weapons training in Tehran, with ballistic missiles displayed at mass weddings. The demonstrations aim to project strength and motivate hardliners amid economic hardship and war uncertainty.

item.title: 🚢 Iran Claims IRGC Coordinated Transit of 26 Vessels Through Hormuz in 24 Hours
item.description: Iran says its Revolutionary Guard is controlling traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, publishing a new map marking a controlled maritime zone. The FAO warned the blockade could trigger a severe global food price crisis within 6-12 months.

item.title: 🇦🇹🕵️ Austrian Ex-Intelligence Officer Found Guilty of Spying for Russia
item.description: Former Austrian intelligence official Egisto Ott was sentenced to over four years in prison for passing information to Russian intelligence and Wirecard fugitive Jan Marsalek. The case has revived concerns about Austria as a hub for Russian espionage.

item.title: 📉 Developed Asia Private Capital Dries Up as Iran War Hits Markets
item.description: Private equity and venture capital activity in developed Asia-Pacific markets dropped significantly as the Iran conflict disrupted global supply chains and investor sentiment.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Europe's Russia sanctions backfire as Moscow pivots to Asia
item.description: Russia has successfully redirected trade and energy exports to Asian markets, undermining European sanctions efforts and strengthening Moscow's economic resilience.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Erdogan Tells Trump Iran Issues Can Be Resolved Through Diplomacy
item.description: Turkish President Tayyip Erdogan welcomed the extension of the US-Iran ceasefire in a call with Trump, saying he believes the contested issues can be resolved. Turkey continues to position itself as a mediator in the conflict.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Erdogan welcomes Iran ceasefire extension, offers mediation
item.description: Turkish President Tayyip Erdogan expressed support for the extended ceasefire between the US and Iran during a call with Trump, stating Turkey is ready to help resolve ongoing disputes.

item.title: 🇬🇧⚔️ Russia dangerously intercepts British spy plane over Black Sea
item.description: Two Russian fighter jets conducted a dangerous intercept of a UK Royal Air Force surveillance aircraft over the Black Sea, the British defense ministry reported, marking a serious escalation in NATO-Russia tensions.

item.title: 🇬🇧⚔️ Russian jets conducted 'dangerous' intercept of British plane over Black Sea
item.description: Britain's defense ministry accused Russia of a dangerous intercept of a RAF surveillance plane over the Black Sea last month, raising concerns about potential NATO-Russia escalation.

item.title: 🇷🇺⚔️ Russia dangerously intercepts British spy plane over Black Sea
item.description: Two Russian jets conducted a dangerous intercept of a British surveillance plane over the Black Sea, Britain's defense ministry confirmed, in an incident that risks escalating NATO-Russia tensions.

item.title: 📈 Iran's stock market reopens after controlled shutdown
item.description: Iran's stock exchange resumed trading after a lengthy shutdown with controlled measures in place, as the country attempts to stabilize its economy amid war with the US.

item.title: 🇮🇷💰 Iran consolidates control over Hormuz with island checkpoints and diplomatic deals
item.description: Iran is establishing checkpoints on islands near the Strait of Hormuz and leveraging diplomatic agreements to strengthen its grip on the strategic waterway, complicating international efforts to keep the passage open.

item.title: 🇮🇷 US-Iran war may escalate or conclude, Trump signals uncertainty
item.description: President Trump said the US may need to attack Iran harder or could wait for a peace deal to emerge, continuing his pattern of mixed messaging on the conflict's trajectory.

item.title: 🇬🇧⛽ UK cancels planned fuel tax rise as Iran war hits household budgets
item.description: Britain will extend a fuel duty cut until the end of the year as the government seeks to ease cost-of-living pressures heightened by the Middle East conflict and rising global energy costs.

item.title: 🦠 US doctor exposed to Ebola patient being monitored in Czech Republic
item.description: A US doctor who came into contact with an Ebola patient is being transferred from Uganda to a hospital in Prague for monitoring, as the Congo outbreak spreads to neighboring Uganda.

item.title: 🦠 American Ebola patient in stable condition in Germany
item.description: A US missionary who contracted Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo is in stable condition at a German hospital, while six other high-risk Americans are being moved from DRC to Germany and Czech Republic.

item.title: 🔫 Iranian public receives basic gun training amid Trump threats
item.description: Iranian civilians are receiving weapons training as the country prepares for potential resumption of conflict with the United States. The training sessions are part of broader mobilization efforts.

item.title: 🇬🇱 US needs to put footprint back on Greenland, Trump envoy says
item.description: US special envoy to Greenland Jeff Landry said Washington needs to rebuild its presence on the Arctic island, reflecting renewed US interest in the strategically important territory.

item.title: 🇱🇧 South Lebanon cultural heritage destroyed in ongoing conflict
item.description: Cultural heritage sites in southern Lebanon are being systematically destroyed, with observers saying the aim appears to be erasing the region's identity amid the broader Middle East conflict.
